steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The authors say it's tempting to treat Birmingham's textile clustering as representative of all industrial agglomeration, then immediately caution against doing something with "this one example." The blank must name what you shouldn't do when you take a single case and draw sweeping conclusions from it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "convening with": means meeting or gathering with. You can't "convene with an example" — the phrase makes no logical sense here. ✗
  • "corroborating": means confirming or supporting with additional evidence. The caution isn't against verifying the Birmingham case — it's against over-relying on it. Wrong direction. ✗
  • "scrutinizing": means examining closely. Scrutinizing a single example is careful and appropriate; the authors aren't warning against close examination. ✗
  • "extrapolating from": means drawing broad conclusions from a limited case. The authors warn against using Birmingham textiles — where knowledge spillovers drove collocation — to represent all industries, because leather clothing lacked those spillovers entirely. That's precisely extrapolating from one example. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
